Off Calendar Dec-18-2018 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion For Order To Apportion And Distribute Funds
Off Calendar Aug-28-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for hearing on the Motion to Apportion and Distribute Funds generated from plaintiffs' survival/wrongful death lawsuit and claims against asbestos bankrupt defendant trusts.
Dept. 514. Judge: Leslie C. Nichols. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Mitch L. Lyon, CSR #6840. Telephone: 415-551-3703.

Geoff T. Sloniker, Esq. appeared to present the issues.
Martha O'Brien, as wrongful death heir, (who appeared via CourtCall) was placed under oath and testified.
Kathleen Land, as wrongful death heir, was present, placed under and testified.
No appearance by Wrongful Death Heirs Siobhan O'Brien, Dennis P. O'Brien, Kevin O'Brien or Christine Anne O'Brien.

The Court, having reviewed the moving papers and having considered the evidence, both oral and documentary, and good cause appearing, RULES that the following are entitled to distribution of all net funds from the survival/wrongful death lawsuit and claims against asbestos bankrupt defendant trusts negotiated after the November 17, 2011 death of Dennis O'Brien in the following proportions as follows:

Estate of Dennis O'Brien - 0%.
Marthe O'Brien - 25%.
Christine Anne O'Brien - 12.5%.
Kevin O'Brien - 12.5%.
Estate Mary Donlan - 12.5%.
Kathleen Land - 12.5%.
Dennis P. O'Brien - 12.5%.
Siobhan O'Brien - 12.5%.

All future net funds derived from settlements entered into after the November 17, 2011 death of Dennis O'Brien shall be distributed in the percentages set forth on pages 2 & 3, paragraph 1 of the Order approving the motion for apportionment.

The signature of plaintiff Kathleen Land alone is sufficient to bind the estate and wrongful death heirs on all settlement documents.

Proposed Order to Apportion and Distribute Funds was submitted to and signed by the Court.

Meeti S. Sudame of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / moving parties Martha O'Brien as wrongful death heir, and as successor-in-interest to Dennis O'Brien, deceased, et al.

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS' MOTION TO CONTINUE TRIAL DATE AND RE-OPEN EXPERT DISCOVERY FOR THE LIMITED PURPOSE OF OFFERING PLAINTIFFS' EXPERT IN PLACE OF DR. SCHWARTZ

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' motion to continue the trial date and reopen expert discovery is granted pursuant to C.C.P. section 2024.050 and CRC 3.1332. Expert discovery is re-opened for the limited purpose of allowing plaintiffs to replace Dr. Levy for Dr. Schwartz. Dr. Levy was timely designated. Dr. Levy may prepare a report and plaintiffs must serve his report and offer him for deposition. Defendants have leave to amend their expert witness designations, limited to responding to Dr. Levy. The June 15, 2015 trial date, with a current return date of December 7, 2015, is continued to February 1, 2016 at 11:15a.m. in Department 503. The limited re-opening of expert discovery closes January 28, 2016. All fact discovery and all other expert discovery remain closed. No party opposes plaintiffs' motion. Proposed order was modified and signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported (503/TLJ)
